mission.title: Install and Configure Holmesgpt on Kubernetes
mission.description: HolmesGPT is an open-source AI agent designed for investigating production incidents and finding root causes. As a CNCF Sandbox project, it integrates with various observability tools and cloud platforms to enhance incident response capabilities.

steps.title: Add the Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official Helm repository for HolmesGPT to your Helm configuration. This will allow you to install the HolmesGPT chart easily.
```bash
helm repo add robusta-charts https://robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install HolmesGPT using Helm
steps.description:
Install the HolmesGPT Helm chart in a new namespace called 'holmesgpt'. This command will deploy the application with the specified version.
```bash
helm install holmes robusta-charts/holmes --namespace holmesgpt --create-namespace --version 0.20.4-alpha
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the HolmesGPT pods are running correctly. This command will list all the pods in the 'holmesgpt' namespace.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace holmesgpt
```

steps.title: Configure resource limits
steps.description:
Update the deployment to set resource limits for the HolmesGPT application. This ensures that the application has the necessary resources allocated.
```bash
kubectl patch deployment holmes --namespace holmesgpt -p '{"spec":{"template":{"spec":{"containers":[{"name":"holmes","resources":{"requests":{"cpu":"100m","memory":"2048Mi"},"limits":{"memory":"2048Mi"}}}]}}}}}'
```

steps.title: Set up port forwarding for access
steps.description:
Forward a local port to the HolmesGPT service to access the application from your local machine.
```bash
kubectl port-forward svc/holmes --namespace holmesgpt 8080:80
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the HolmesGPT Helm release from your cluster. This command will remove the application and its associated resources.
```bash
helm uninstall holmes --namespace holmesgpt
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs) that were created during the installation. This ensures that all resources related to HolmesGPT are removed.
```bash
kubectl delete crd holmesgpts.robusta.dev
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'holmesgpt' namespace to clean up any remaining resources associated with the installation.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace holmesgpt
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, it's a good practice to back up the current state of the deployment. You can do this by exporting the current configuration.
```bash
kubectl get all --namespace holmesgpt -o yaml > holmesgpt-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Make sure your Helm repository is up to date to get the latest chart versions.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Helm release
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the HolmesGPT release to the latest version available in the repository.
```bash
helm upgrade holmes robusta-charts/holmes --namespace holmesgpt --version 0.20.4-alpha
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check the status of the pods to ensure that the upgrade was successful and that all pods are running correctly.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace holmesgpt
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in Pending state
troubleshooting.description:
If your pods are stuck in a Pending state, it may be due to insufficient resources. Check the events for the namespace to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl get events --namespace holmesgpt
```

troubleshooting.title: Service not reachable
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the service, ensure that the port forwarding is set up correctly and that the service is running. Check the service status with:
```bash
kubectl get svc --namespace holmesgpt
```

troubleshooting.title: CrashLoopBackOff error
troubleshooting.description:
If you see a CrashLoopBackOff error, check the logs of the pod to identify the issue. This can provide insights into what is causing the pod to crash.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> --namespace holmesgpt
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of HolmesGPT will have all pods running in the 'holmesgpt' namespace, and you will be able to access the application via the port you forwarded. Configuration changes should be applied as needed to optimize performance.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add robusta-charts https://robusta-charts.storage.googleapis.com
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and Helm installed on your local machine.
